# CalibrateDS

> A local-first design system compiler and quality control toolkit for design engineers. Connects Figma to code, enforces design freshness, and automates React component generation.

## What CalibrateDS does

CalibrateDS is a CLI tool (`@calibrate-ds/cli`) and Figma plugin that bridges the gap between design and engineering. It reads a Figma file, normalises it into a structured DesignSystemModel, and generates typed React components, design tokens, and AI-ready implementation prompts.

Key capabilities:
- Scans Figma files and builds a normalised DesignSystemModel (variants, tokens, states, interactions)
- Generates typed React + TypeScript components from the model
- MCP (Model Context Protocol) server — lets AI assistants like Claude query live design context directly
- `ptb status` — detects design drift: which components are stale vs. fresh
- `ptb dev` — live Vite playground to preview generated components with variant controls sourced from Figma
- Figma plugin with 6 quality modules: Accessibility Advisor, Component Readiness, Token Inspector, State Diff Engine, Stress Test, Token Exporter

## Why it exists

Design systems drift. Engineers implement components once and never update them when designers iterate. CalibrateDS treats your Figma file as source code — every component has a `contextHash` that changes when the design changes, making staleness measurable and automatable in CI.

## How to install

```bash
npm install -g @calibrate-ds/cli
ptb init
```

## Links

- Documentation: https://calibrateds.deepadalja.com/docs/getting-started/quickstart
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@calibrate-ds/cli

- MCP setup: https://calibrateds.deepadalja.com/docs/mcp/setup
